items.title: Medicaid/CHIP enrollment fell to 74.3 million as of March 2026 (CMS-reported data)
items.summary: KFF’s tracker of CMS Performance Indicator Project data shows 74.3 million people enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP nationally as of March 2026, including 67.1 million in Medicaid and 7.2 million in CHIP. The tracker notes Medicaid enrollment declined by 4.8 million people (6%) from March 2025 to March 2026.

items.title: Medicaid work-requirement implementation: federal start set for Jan. 1, 2027; some states moved earlier
items.summary: KFF’s tracker says the 2025 reconciliation law requires Medicaid work requirements beginning Jan. 1, 2027. The tracker also lists earlier state start dates, including Georgia on May 1, 2026; Tennessee and Utah on July 1, 2026; and Wisconsin on Dec. 1, 2026.

items.title: Proposed CMS rule would cap some Medicaid directed payments at Medicare rates; CMS estimates $510B federal reduction (2026–2035)
items.summary: KFF reports the 2025 reconciliation law created new limits on Medicaid state directed payments (SDPs) for inpatient and outpatient hospital services, nursing facility services, and professional services at academic medical centers. The caps are set at 100% of Medicare rates in Medicaid expansion states and 110% in non-expansion states, and KFF notes CMS estimated the proposed rule would reduce federal Medicaid spending by $510 billion between 2026 and 2035.

items.title: USDA: FY 2025 SNAP payment error rate was 10.62% (about $10.1B in improper payments); states at/above 6% face future cost-sharing
items.summary: USDA reported a national SNAP payment error rate of 10.62% for FY 2025 and estimated $10.1 billion in improper payments (overpayments and underpayments combined). USDA said states with error rates at or above 6% will be responsible for covering 5%, 10%, or 15% of their state’s benefits, in most cases starting Oct. 1, 2027.

items.title: USDA opened SNAP Fraud Framework Implementation Grants (up to $5 million); applications close July 17, 2026
items.summary: USDA announced SNAP Fraud Framework Implementation Grants to support states in preventing, detecting, and responding to recipient fraud. The funding opportunity offers up to $5 million and the application window runs from June 15, 2026 through July 17, 2026.

items.title: FAFSA 2026–27 federal deadline is June 30, 2027; corrections accepted until Sept. 12, 2027
items.summary: Federal Student Aid lists the 2026–27 FAFSA submission deadline as June 30, 2027 at 11:59 p.m. Central time. The same table lists a separate deadline for corrections or updates of Sept. 12, 2027 at 11:59 p.m. Central time.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)

items.title: HUD set Jan. 1, 2027 as the compliance deadline for key HOTMA changes for most voucher and public housing programs
items.summary: The National Apartment Association reports HUD set Jan. 1, 2027 as the compliance deadline for all aspects of HOTMA sections 102 and 104 for PHAs administering Housing Choice Voucher (HCV), Project-Based Voucher, Moderate Rehabilitation, Moderate Rehabilitation SRO, and public housing programs. The same summary notes exclusions for Moving to Work (MTW) PHAs and PHAs that exclusively use the HUD Family Reporting Software.

items.title: IRS: Free File remains available through Oct. 15; guided filing available for AGI $89,000 or less
items.summary: In a June 2, 2026 release, the IRS said taxpayers with adjusted gross income (AGI) of $89,000 or less can use IRS Free File guided software to file a federal return (and usually a state return) at no cost. The IRS said Free File remains available through Oct. 15.
